salientSummary: Scaffolded xcodegen-driven Xcode project for Kite (nl.rb2.kite, macOS 15+, Swift 5.9, ad-hoc Sign to Run Locally, sandbox off, Hardened Runtime off). @main KiteApp boots into an empty three-pane NavigationSplitView with Settings scene. KiteTests (Swift Testing) + KiteUITests (XCTest) smoke targets, Info.plist with all required keys, swiftformat/swiftlint configs, .gitignore. Debug build SUCCEEDS with zero warnings; swiftformat --lint and swiftlint --strict pass; Swift Testing smoke suite passes. XCUITest runner failed to initialize due to a host-machine TCC/Automation permission (NOT a code issue); documented below.
whatWasImplemented: project.yml (xcodegen spec: 3 targets -- app + unit-test + UI-test, deployment macOS 15.0, Swift 5.9, ad-hoc signing, Info.plist + entitlements wired). Info.plist with CFBundleIdentifier=nl.rb2.kite, CFBundleName=Kite, CFBundleDisplayName=Kite, CFBundleShortVersionString=0.1.0, CFBundleVersion=1, LSApplicationCategoryType=public.app-category.developer-tools, LSMinimumSystemVersion=15.0, NSHighResolutionCapable=true. Kite.entitlements with com.apple.security.app-sandbox=false. Sources/App/KiteApp.swift (@main App with WindowGroup, Settings scene, .windowResizability(.contentSize), 900x600 min). Sources/App/Commands.swift (stub KiteCommands builder; real shortcuts arrive in M8). Sources/Views/RootView.swift (NavigationSplitView with sidebar 'Repos' | content 'Branches and graph' | detail 'Diff', column widths set, accessibility identifiers). Resources/Assets.xcassets with placeholder AppIcon slot (icon populated in M8-app-icon-and-plist). Tests/KiteTests/SmokeTest.swift (Swift Testing suite: @Test for bundle id prefix + @Test arithmetic). Tests/KiteUITests/SmokeUITest.swift (XCUITest launching XCUIApplication + waiting for main window). .swiftformat (v5.9 rules), .swiftlint.yml (relaxed line length 140, disabled trailing_comma/todo/opening_brace), .gitignore (Kite.xcodeproj, DerivedData, build, .swiftpm, Package.resolved, xcuserdata, .DS_Store). README.md expanded with prereqs, quickstart, DEVELOPER_DIR override, mission pointer. Deliberately skipped Sources/Design/Palette.swift (no consumers yet -- avoiding dead files; later feature can add it). Deliberately deferred pointfreeco/swift-snapshot-testing SPM dependency (comment left in project.yml; first snapshot-using feature will attach it to its target).
whatWasLeftUndone: swift-snapshot-testing SPM dependency intentionally deferred (no consumers yet; avoids an unused Package.resolved). Sources/Design/Palette.swift skipped per the task's explicit 'skip if you don't need it yet' guidance. AppIcon artwork is a placeholder (empty set) -- real .icns ships in M8-app-icon-and-plist which owns that slot.

commandsRun.observation: ** BUILD SUCCEEDED ** with zero warnings; Kite.app signed ad-hoc; Info.plist in bundle contains all required keys
** TEST SUCCEEDED ** -- Swift Testing: 2 of 2 tests in 'Kite scaffold smoke tests' suite passed (0.001s)

** TEST FAILED ** -- NOT a code defect. KiteUITests-Runner failed to initialize with 'Failed to initialize for UI testing: com.apple.LocalAuthentication Code=-4 "System authentication is running."' on first attempt and 'Timed out while enabling automation mode.' on retry. This is a host-machine TCC/Automation permission issue: the XCUITest runner requires macOS Accessibility/Automation entitlements granted to Xcode/testmanagerd via System Settings -> Privacy & Security, which is an interactive user grant that cannot be done from CI/code. Unit tests (Swift Testing) ran fine in the same xcodebuild invocation. See interactiveChecks below for manual-launch confirmation. Flagged as a non-blocking discovered issue for user-testing-validator-foundation to re-run once Automation permission is granted.
0/5 files require formatting; config at .swiftformat honored

Info.plist in built bundle shows CFBundleIdentifier=nl.rb2.kite, CFBundleName=Kite, CFBundleDisplayName=Kite, CFBundleShortVersionString=0.1.0, CFBundleVersion=1, LSApplicationCategoryType=public.app-category.developer-tools, LSMinimumSystemVersion=15.0, NSHighResolutionCapable=true -- fulfills VAL-PKG-004
Identifier=nl.rb2.kite, Format=app bundle with Mach-O thin (arm64), Signature=adhoc, TeamIdentifier=not set -- matches Sign to Run Locally target

interactiveChecks.action: Ran `open /Users/ruben/Library/Developer/Xcode/DerivedData/Kite-fngogvmrmaqbotclxsurqtoelbkr/Build/Products/Debug/Kite.app`, waited 3 seconds, ran `pgrep -x Kite`, then `pkill -x Kite`
interactiveChecks.observed: pgrep returned PID 22260 (Kite was alive and running). The app launched successfully from the built bundle. A follow-up osascript window-title query failed with AppleEvent timeout (-1712) because this machine has not granted Automation permission to Terminal/osascript -- the same environmental limitation that blocks XCUITest below. The app itself launched, registered with Launch Services, and held a process; killing it cleanly via pkill confirmed it was responsive to signals. Full visual confirmation of the three-pane NavigationSplitView (sidebar / content / detail placeholders) should be verified by the user at the next in-person session -- the automated path is blocked by the Automation permission.

interactiveChecks.action: XCUITest smoke test target built but runner could not initialize
interactiveChecks.observed: Documented above under commandsRun. The KiteUITests-Runner.app is code-signed correctly and present at /Users/ruben/Library/Developer/Xcode/DerivedData/Kite-fngogvmrmaqbotclxsurqtoelbkr/Build/Products/Debug/. Once the user grants Xcode (or /Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer/usr/bin/testmanagerd) Accessibility permission in System Settings, the smoke test should pass. VAL-UI-001 is fulfillable against the same codebase; subsequent milestones that add XCUITest coverage (M2+) will need this permission resolved.

cases.name: test bundle loads and host app bundle identifier is present

discoveredIssues.description: XCUITest runner fails to initialize on this machine with 'Timed out while enabling automation mode' / 'System authentication is running' errors. Root cause is a host-level TCC/Automation permission that was not granted to Xcode's test runner -- it is an interactive system-settings grant the user must perform once (System Settings -> Privacy & Security -> Automation/Accessibility, add Xcode or grant the prompt on first test run). This blocks ALL XCUITest-based VAL checks across the mission (VAL-REPO-*, VAL-UI-*, etc.) until resolved. Swift Testing (KiteTests) runs fine in the same xcodebuild invocation, so subsequent git-engine / parser / persistence features can still ship their unit test coverage. The first feature that owns an XCUITest check (M2-repo-scan's VAL-REPO-001 via KiteUITests/RepoScanTests) should be preceded by a 'please grant Automation permission to Xcode' prompt from the orchestrator.
discoveredIssues.affectsFeature: user-testing-validator-foundation, M2-repo-sidebar, and every later milestone with XCUITest coverage

discoveredIssues.description: SwiftFormat 0.61.0 requires --lint to appear AFTER path arguments ('swiftformat Sources Tests --lint'), not before. The task doc's example 'swiftformat --lint Sources Tests' errors with 'argument does not expect a value'. Updated invocation in this feature's verification and mentioned in README so future features get it right.
discoveredIssues.affectsFeature: ALL future features -- they should use 'swiftformat Sources Tests --lint' when running the validation gate

discoveredIssues.description: The task brief states xcode-select points at /Library/Developer/CommandLineTools. On this host it actually already points at /Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer. DEVELOPER_DIR export is still harmless and keeps the workflow portable; documented in README.
